content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: NOTE_WRITE
        Router memory types: NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic note/source/entity route; hard claims/actionItems can be produced by later consolidation.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory model layers and design goals; The v3 data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by splitting memory into layers: Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit).; scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; `Note` entity role, boundaries, and invariants in v3; `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment used for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims; it excludes source records and chain-of-thought, and follows invariants like one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning.; scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md)
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; `Entity` as canonical memory node with alias/merge semantics; In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; it uses aliases and merge status while preserving history, keeping aliases pointing to the current canonical entity, and allowing erroneous merges to be reversed via audit trail.; scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<3> DIRECTION; Cross-cutting policy: scope, retrieval lifecycle, telemetry, and retention; In v3, scope and retrieval lifecycle are explicit cross-cutting concerns: use dual scope fields (`scope_text` for explainability and `scope_json` for retrieval/reconciliation), keep `truth status` separate from `retrieval_state`, apply retrieval states active/warm/cold/archived/deleted, track usage fields (`use_count`, `last_used_at`, `last_validated_at`), and enforce explicit retention policy: low-use notes can move to `cold`, old resolved items to `archived`, sensitive source data supports soft/hard delete on request, and historical claims may remain explainable while being deprioritized in top retrieval. Recommended retention metadata includes `retention_class`, `expires_at`, `deleted_at`, and `archived_at`.; scope=Applies to cross-cutting fields and retention policy in '02. Data Model / 1..2 and 6' of agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<4> DIRECTION; `MemoryRun` schema guidance and invariants (`run log` retention, `input_hash` idempotency); Section 3.9 provides recommended `MemoryRun` fields (identity, run metadata, retrieval inputs, prompt/model versions, outputs, applied/repair ops, latency/tokens, status/errors, timestamp) and defines invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` supports idempotency and retry safety.; scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<5> DIRECTION; Claim and Note serve complementary roles in memory; The section defines a deliberate split: `Claim` is for precise, reconcilable, time-aware factual usage, while `Note` is for richer contextual meaning (decision history, rationale, evolving meaning) with later consolidation. Overusing only one type causes imbalance: false precision if everything is a `Claim`, or overly soft memory if everything is a `Note`.; scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
- note hot-path:note:<6> DOC_DIGEST; `PredicateCatalog` is a reconciliation config, not a memory item; In the v3 data model, `PredicateCatalog` is defined as configuration for reconciliation logic rather than a stored memory item; it encodes per-predicate rules such as cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and projection sync flags.; scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md)
- note hot-path:note:<7> DOC_DIGEST; `Profile` is a compact, rebuildable projection layer; In section 3.6, `Profile` is defined as an always-in-context projection, not a source of truth, built from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should stay short, prioritize explicit facts, keep disputable facts in claim/note, and remain rebuildable.; scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.6 `Profile` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=v3; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=v3; reason=Section refers to the same v3 memory data model context as existing concept entity.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=02. Data Model|agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md|02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=02. Data Model agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке MARKDOWN Document title: 02. Data Model Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке Document lines: 619-628 ## 8. Модель данных в одной строке - `Source` = что было сказано / увидено - `Entity` = кто/что это вообще - `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт - `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом - `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста - `Task` = что должно быть сделано - `Episode` = чему система научилась - `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась
        predicate_hints=

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:8fa55469a066ca10b666d96d6b7c1f26] EXTERNAL
Source metadata: source_kind=document; origin=pasted_document_section; document_type=MARKDOWN; source_ref=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md; title=02. Data Model; heading=02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке; imported_at=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
